subject:
M 1.0.9 crashes while refine at the "reconstructing and filtering
 xx" step
body:
Dear all,
I followed the tutorial and imported the data source and species 
successfully. But every time I click "refine",  it crashes. After many 
trials, I followed the "disabling all refinement options except 'particle 
poses'." suggestion in 1 post and it still crashes.  I observed that it 
could finish a few steps but crash during the final "reconstructing and 
filtering xx" step.
Following are the error messages:

[image: M_refine_error_message1.png][image: M_refine_error_message2.png][image: 
M_refine_error_message3.png]

11/15/2022 3:50:21 PM:

System.InvalidOperationException: Dialog isn't visible to close

   at 
MahApps.Metro.Controls.Dialogs.ProgressDialogController.<CloseAsync>b__33_0()

   at MahApps.Metro.Controls.Dialogs.ProgressDialogController.CloseAsync()

   at M.MainWindow.<PerformRefinementIteration>d__30.MoveNext() in 
D:\Dev\warp2\M\MainWindow.xaml.cs:line 907

--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---

   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()

   at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.InternalRealCall(Delegate 
callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs)

   at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.TryCatchWhen(Object source, 
Delegate callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs, Delegate catchHandler)

   at 
MahApps.Metro.Controls.Dialogs.ProgressDialogController.<CloseAsync>b__33_0()

   at MahApps.Metro.Controls.Dialogs.ProgressDialogController.CloseAsync()

   at M.MainWindow.<PerformRefinementIteration>d__30.MoveNext() in 
D:\Dev\warp2\M\MainWindow.xaml.cs:line 907

--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---

   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()

   at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.InternalRealCall(Delegate 
callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs)

   at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.TryCatchWhen(Object source, 
Delegate callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs, Delegate catchHandler)


We are using a RTX 2080 Ti graphics card. Looking forward to help and 
suggestions. Thanks very much!

Best,

Miffy
